verdicts.reason: Sink is array_filter() with a PHP closure callback — not a shell execution function. array_filter() does not execute OS commands. The taint label 'cmd' is a false scanner label. No RCE is possible here.
verdicts.auth_context: Authenticated admin — irrelevant since there is no vulnerability

verdicts.reason: Identical pattern to rce-001. Sink is array_filter() with a closure — not an OS command sink. No RCE possible.
Identical pattern to rce-001/002. Sink is array_filter() with closure. No OS command execution. False scanner label.

Sink is array_filter() with a static closure checking $item == 1. This is standard PHP array filtering — no OS command execution or code evaluation. Source is post_meta read during upgrade routine. No RCE.

The echo statement in system-status.php is inside cmplz_get_system_status() which is only called when cmplz_user_can_manage() returns true (line 87 of system-status.php). The output is also delivered as a plain-text file download (Content-type: application/octet-stream), not rendered as HTML in the browser. No XSS.

Same function as xss-001. The output of implode_array_recursive($wizard) is inside cmplz_get_system_status() which is gated by cmplz_user_can_manage() and served as application/octet-stream file download, not HTML. No browser-rendered XSS.

Sink is array_filter() with a closure checking $script['enable'] == 1. This is standard array filtering with no OS command execution. Source is the custom-scripts option which is only writable by admins.
Identical to rce-005. Sink is array_filter() with closure. Not an OS command sink. No RCE.
Sink is array_filter() with a closure checking enable_placeholder and iframe fields. Not an OS command. No RCE.
array_filter() with closure on custom-scripts option. Not a shell sink. No RCE.
Multi-step array_filter/array_merge chain on custom-scripts — all PHP data structure operations, no OS command sink. No RCE.
array_filter() on whitelist_script. Not a shell sink. No RCE.

The URL is constructed as WSC_SCANNER_ENDPOINT (a hardcoded constant) + '/api/v1/scans/' + sanitize_text_field($scan_id). The scan_id is a string identifier stored by the admin during scan initiation. The attacker cannot control the base URL (WSC_SCANNER_ENDPOINT). The appended scan_id is just a path suffix to a fixed external endpoint — this is not SSRF. No arbitrary host/scheme control.
Duplicate of ssrf-001. The taint source here is sanitize_text_field($scan_id) — sanitized already. URL construction appends to a hardcoded constant endpoint. No SSRF.

The sink is array_map() with a sanitizing closure — each error array element is sanitize_text_field()'d. This is a sanitation operation, not a code execution sink. The scanner mistakenly labels array_map with a callback as RCE. No vulnerability.

The URL is WSC_ENDPOINT + '/api/lite/users' — a hardcoded constant. The data fields in the body (email, base_url, partner) are sent as POST body parameters, not as the URL. send_auth_email() is gated by cmplz_user_can_manage(). The scanner incorrectly flagged the request body construction as SSRF. No SSRF.

The URL is WSC_ENDPOINT + '/api/lite/oauth_applications' — hardcoded constant. The token is sent in the POST body as a JSON field. confirm_email_auth() is gated by cmplz_user_can_manage() at line 207. The GET parameter token cannot redirect the request to an arbitrary host. No SSRF.

URL is CONS_ENDPOINT — a hardcoded constant class property. The email is sent in the POST body JSON, not as the URL. The function store_onboarding_consent() is called from cron hooks or admin flows. No attacker-controlled URL. No SSRF.
Duplicate of ssrf-005. NEWSLETTER_SIGNUP_ENDPOINT is a hardcoded constant. Email is POST body parameter. No SSRF.

The source here is a locally constructed $api_params array containing sanitize_email($email) and esc_url_raw(site_url()). The destination URL is NEWSLETTER_SIGNUP_ENDPOINT — a hardcoded class constant. No attacker-controlled URL. No SSRF.

Sink is array_filter() with a closure comparing $value !== $client_credentials. This is a standard PHP array filtering operation to remove a matching credential from a retry list. No OS command execution. No RCE. The critical rating is entirely unwarranted.
verdicts.auth_context: Called from cron (cmplz_every_day_hook) — not directly attacker-reachable

verdicts.reason: The sink is uasort($fields, callback) — a PHP array sort function. uasort() does not execute OS commands. The function cmplz_fields() is gated by cmplz_user_can_manage() at line 67. The scanner mislabeled uasort as a code execution sink. No vulnerability.

process_ajax_activate_license() is gated by current_user_can('activate_plugins') and requires a valid nonce (wp_verify_nonce on token). The license is sanitized with sanitize_title(). The response is json_encode($response) where $response comes from the EDD API — it echoes a JSON-encoded array, not a raw XSS vector. No exploitable XSS.

The request URL is $this->api_url — a class property set to the EDD store URL (hardcoded constant, not user-supplied). The license key is sent as a POST body parameter, not as part of the URL. Requires activate_plugins + nonce. No SSRF.

The rename() call uses $new_dir from get_transient('cmplz_upgrade_dir') and renames it to $default_dir (the plugin's own directory). The transient is set internally during the upgrade process, not from user input. Called within process_ajax_activate_license() which requires activate_plugins + nonce. Transient poisoning would require admin-level database write. No realistic attack path.

Sink is array_filter() with closure checking $script['enable'] == 1. Standard array filtering. No RCE. The option complianz_options_custom-scripts is written only through the authenticated integrations REST action (cmplz_user_can_manage + nonce-verified cmplz_do_action).
Source is $wpdb->get_results() reading from the cmplz_cookies table. Sink is array_filter() with a closure. Standard filtering — no OS command execution. No RCE.
verdicts.auth_context: Called during frontend cookie banner rendering

verdicts.reason: Same as rce-015. Two sequential array_filter() calls on cookie DB results. No OS command execution. No RCE.
verdicts.auth_context: Called during frontend cookie banner rendering

unauthenticated_rest_endpoint_wsc_scan.analysis: The endpoint has no WP authentication but implements its own validation: (1) requires cmplz_wsc_scan_id + cmplz_wsc_scan_createdAt options to exist (only set when admin initiates a scan), (2) requires User-Agent header containing 'radar', (3) requires body JSON event == 'scan-completed'. If all pass, it calls wsc_complete_cookie_scan() which stores trackers in a transient via set_transient(). Cookie data is subsequently sanitized in wsc_scan_store_cookies() using sanitize_text_field() before DB insert. The weak User-Agent check ('radar' in string) combined with the scan_id requirement provides marginal security. If an attacker knows a site has an active scan, they could call this with User-Agent: radar and inject cookie names — but sanitize_text_field() prevents XSS/SQLi. LOW residual risk — not exploitable for meaningful impact.

unauthenticated_rest_endpoint_wsc_checks.analysis: Same validation as wsc-scan but for checks data. Calls wsc_scan_process_checks() which stores result in set_transient('cmplz_wsc_last_checks'). Data is accessed only by admin pages. No XSS/RCE path identified.

custom_scripts_editor_field.analysis: The 'editor' field in complianz_options_custom-scripts is saved without sanitization in sanitize_scripts() (line 317: $scripts[$type][$key]['editor'] = $value['editor'] — no sanitize call). This value is later echoed verbatim on the frontend in class-banner-loader.php line 591: echo $script['editor']. However, the write path (update_scripts action) requires: (1) cmplz_user_can_manage() check in integrations_data(), which calls cmplz_do_action(), (2) cmplz_do_action() requires wp_verify_nonce($nonce, 'cmplz_react') and cmplz_user_can_manage(). Since only users with manage_privacy (admin-equivalent) can save this, this is an 'admin can store JS' scenario — which is standard WordPress behavior and not a security vulnerability per WordPress security policy. NOT a vulnerability unless the site separates admin roles with custom capabilities that exclude manage_privacy from trusted.

additional_investigation_notes.false_positive_pattern_summary: The scanner systematically mislabeled array_filter(), array_map(), and uasort() with PHP closures as 'RCE' sinks because it tracks the 'cmd' taint label without understanding that these are PHP data-structure operations, not shell execution functions. All SSRF flags were false because the destination URLs are hardcoded class constants — user data only flows into POST body parameters, not into the host/scheme portion of URLs.
